summary: Kenneth Suna discusses a four-fund passive investment allocation approach (S&P 500, dividends, tech, REITs) with example portfolio weightings for different investor risk profiles. This is foundational index fund allocation strategy unrelated to options trading or premium generation.
takeaways: Four-fund allocation model: S&P 500 Index Fund, Dividend ETF, Tech ETF, Real Estate Investment Trust
Example conservative allocation: 25% each across four funds
Example growth-focused allocation: 60% Tech, 20% S&P 500, 15% Dividend, 5% REITs
Investment allocation is personal and depends on risk tolerance and age
No single prescribed allocation fits all investors
title: Investing by Percent
